meta.lock_note: Gold FROZEN by the tester BEFORE any producer run. The producer MUST be GOLD-BLIND — a fresh session that has not seen this file — else the eval grades its own homework.
meta.purpose: Hand-picked-HARD to expose failure modes. Report PER-K-MODE pass rate + a PESSIMISTIC lower-bound accuracy. This is NOT an expected-production-% — that comes from the separate representative random sample (pass4_representative_sample.*).
meta.no_producer_run_until: tester locks this file + the computed coverage matrix

scoring_rubric.per_entry: recall = gold expected_drivers found (name OK if exact OR in allowed_alternatives; state OK via state_synonym_scoring_map; direction exact). precision = no driver coined that is in expected_rejections AND, for reject-only entries, ZERO drivers coined.
scoring_rubric.state_scoring: match if producer state == gold state OR producer state in state_synonym_scoring_map[gold_state]. NO fuzzy string match. The map is SCORING-ONLY -- NOT producer write-vocabulary; the producer emits canonical STATES_VOCAB tokens only.
scoring_rubric.name_scoring: exact gold name OR allowed_alternatives. A name embedding state/period/magnitude/ticker = FAIL (and should appear in expected_rejections).
scoring_rubric.allowed_outcomes: gold.allowed_outcomes lists acceptable OUTCOMES from {admit, defer, reject}. DEFAULT when absent: entries WITH expected_drivers => [admit]; reject-only entries => [reject]. 'defer' is an OUTCOME, NEVER a driver-name alternative.
scoring_rubric.defer_vs_admit: entries whose allowed_outcomes include both 'admit' and 'defer' (K13 singletons) score CORRECT if the producer EITHER admits the listed driver OR defers -- calibration, not a forced label.
scoring_rubric.headline: stress_accuracy = mean per-entry F1; ALSO per-K-mode pass rate. stress_accuracy is a LOWER BOUND, not production accuracy.

failure_mode_taxonomy.K1: reuse — same driver recurs across events (must REUSE, not re-propose)
failure_mode_taxonomy.K3: semantic-near-dup — same concept, LOW lexical overlap (dedup-surface stress)
failure_mode_taxonomy.K4: state-in-name trap — direction word sits inside the cause
failure_mode_taxonomy.K5: direction-flip — same driver, opposite direction across events
failure_mode_taxonomy.K6: one-off-reject — event-specific item, NOT a reusable driver
failure_mode_taxonomy.K7: boilerplate/macro-reject — sounds important, is not a company driver
failure_mode_taxonomy.K9: multi-driver — multiple drivers in one snippet
failure_mode_taxonomy.K10: mixed-direction same-event — drivers diverge in direction
failure_mode_taxonomy.K11: cross-entity — the cause belongs to ANOTHER company
failure_mode_taxonomy.K12: messy-long — driver buried among irrelevant facts / hedging
failure_mode_taxonomy.K13: singleton defer-vs-admit — real but rare; defer OR admit both acceptable
failure_mode_taxonomy.K15: paraphrase-evidence — canonical token NOT literal in evidence (R11 stress)

meta.coverage_matrix_note: Each entry carries `k_modes`; the matrix is COMPUTED from those (validator asserts each K>=3). Do not hand-maintain.
meta.provenance_note: source_catalog SRC:* = verbatim real graph text. SYNTHETIC:* = a DELIBERATELY constructed edge case (realistic financial wording) for a failure mode natural sampling will not reliably surface; flagged for honesty.

corpus.evidence_text: iPhone sales in China are off to a bad start in 2024. iPhone shipments in the Chinese market continued to decline year-on-year.

gold.reference_context_note: iPhone China sales fell early 2024.

corpus.evidence_text: Apple's iPhone sales in China nosedived 19.1% in the first three months of 2024, largely due to the impact of Huawei Technologies Co.'s new product launches in the premium segment.

gold.reference_context_note: iPhone China fell 19% as Huawei took premium share (cause attributed to a rival, but the DRIVER is Apple's own iphone_china_sales).

corpus.evidence_text: Greater China was up 38% year on year. It was driven by iPhone, where we set an all-time revenue record. It's driven by the customer enthusiasm for the iPhone 17 lineup.

gold.reference_context_note: Direction FLIP vs 2024 + sales~revenue synonym: same driver, opposite state.

corpus.evidence_text: iPhone drives record September quarter revenue. Services revenue reaches new all-time high.

gold.reference_context_note: Services all-time high; iPhone record. No 'record'/magnitude in the name.

corpus.evidence_text: Diluted earnings per share was $1.64, up 12 percent year over year when excluding the one-time charge related to the reversal of the European General Court's State Aid decision.

gold.reference_context_note: A one-time EU tax charge — event-specific, NOT a reusable driver.

corpus.evidence_text: We had a decrease in the product gross margin by 340 basis points sequentially, primarily driven by mix, seasonal loss of leverage, foreign exchange, partly offset by cost savings.

gold.reference_context_note: Gross margin -340bps on mix/FX/deleverage.

corpus.evidence_text: We exited the December quarter with very lean channel inventory due to that staggering level of demand. We're in a supply chase mode to meet the very high levels of customer demand. The constraints we have are driven by the availability of the advanced nodes that our SOCs are produced on.

gold.reference_context_note: Supply-constrained by advanced-node availability amid strong demand. K3 pairs with s08 (same concept, different words).

corpus.evidence_text: Component availability remains the binding limit on how many units we can ship; we simply cannot build to the level of orders we are seeing right now.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED low-overlap near-dup of s07: 'component availability binding limit' vs 'advanced node availability' -> SAME supply_constraint driver. Tests dedup catches zero-lexical-overlap equivalence.

corpus.evidence_text: Record quarterly Data Center revenue of $26.3 billion, up 16% from Q1 and up 154% from a year ago.

gold.reference_context_note: Data Center record; no magnitude in name.

corpus.evidence_text: Gross margin 75.1% versus 78.4%, down 3.3 pts sequentially.

gold.reference_context_note: Margin fell 3.3pts despite record revenue (state word 'down' in the cause).

corpus.evidence_text: The networking business was down about 15% sequentially, but your comments were that you were seeing very strong demand. So this quarter is just a slight dip down, and we're going to be right back up. They're getting ready for Blackwell.

gold.reference_context_note: Networking dipped 15% (Blackwell transition); state word in cause.

corpus.evidence_text: Our InfiniBand business grew 5x year over year. Right now we are ramping H200s, there's no way we can reasonably keep up on demand in the short term as we ramp.

gold.reference_context_note: InfiniBand (a networking synonym) grew 5x; demand>supply.

corpus.evidence_text: Fourth-quarter Gaming revenue was $2.5 billion, down 22% from the previous quarter and down 11% from a year ago.

gold.reference_context_note: Gaming -22% sequentially.

corpus.evidence_text: Demand for its newest AI chips continues to accelerate at a pace that may drive total revenue for its Blackwell and Rubin platforms past the previously announced $500 billion target through 2026.

gold.reference_context_note: Novel tokens Blackwell/Rubin; canonical 'datacenter_revenue' is a PARAPHRASE (not literal in text) -> R11 stress.

corpus.evidence_text: AWS segment sales increased 19% year-over-year to $27.5 billion.
International segment operating income was $1.3 billion, compared with an operating loss of $0.1 billion in third quarter 2023.

gold.reference_context_note: Swing from loss to income; 'loss' sits in the prior-period comparator.

corpus.evidence_text: Net sales increased 9% to $155.7 billion. Excluding the $1.4 billion unfavorable impact from year-over-year changes in foreign exchange rates, net sales increased 10%.

gold.reference_context_note: FX a $1.4B headwind (a macro factor that IS a recurring driver).

corpus.evidence_text: Every single one of our advertising offerings this quarter grew in a meaningful way, using our own prime video and live sports capabilities, as well as going all the way down to the bottom of the funnel at point of sale, being able to use sponsored products, it just all leads to a return on advertising spend that's very unusual.

gold.reference_context_note: Long messy passage; the driver is advertising_revenue, buried among Prime Video / live sports / sponsored-products detail.

corpus.evidence_text: The way the AWS business works, the way the cash cycle works, is that the faster we grow, the more CapEx we end up spending because we have to procure data center and hardware and chips and networking gear ahead of when we're able to monetize it. We don't procure it unless we see significant signals of demand.

gold.reference_context_note: Capex rising on AI demand; 'capex' is paraphrased ('the more CapEx we end up spending'), buried in a long cash-cycle explanation.

corpus.evidence_text: Semiconductor revenue was a record $30.1 billion driven by AI revenue of $12.2 billion. AI revenue which grew 220 percent year-on-year was driven by our leading AI XPUs and Ethernet networking portfolio.

gold.reference_context_note: 'XPU' = AVGO's novel token for AI accelerators.

corpus.evidence_text: Q1 AI revenue grew 77% year-over-year to $4.1 billion and infrastructure software revenue grew 47% year-over-year to $6.7 billion.

gold.reference_context_note: AI +77% and infra software +47% (VMware).

corpus.evidence_text: Meta's first-quarter results revealed a significant increase in capital spending, now forecast to reach as high as $72 billion for the full year, largely driven by investments in AI and data centers. Broadcom plays a critical role in powering hyperscale data centers through its networking chips and custom silicon.

gold.reference_context_note: CROSS-ENTITY: the +$72B capex is META's; for AVGO the driver is hyperscaler_capex (demand tailwind), NOT 'meta_capex'.

corpus.evidence_text: We have $73 billion of backlog in place of XPUs, switches, DSPs, lasers for AI data centers that we anticipate shipping over the next 18 months, and the bookings have been accelerating.

gold.reference_context_note: $73B AI backlog accelerating.

corpus.evidence_text: 1Q deliveries has been a nightmare quarter for Tesla as China demand remains very soft coming out of the gates for 2024.

gold.reference_context_note: Q1 deliveries fell on soft China demand.

corpus.evidence_text: We recognized record regulatory credit revenues in Q2 as other OEMs are still behind on meeting emissions requirements.

gold.reference_context_note: Record reg-credit revenue.

corpus.evidence_text: $1.6B GAAP operating income in Q2 after restructuring and other charges of $0.6B.

gold.reference_context_note: A $0.6B restructuring charge — event-specific, not a reusable driver.

corpus.evidence_text: We continue to review every aspect of our cost of goods sold (COGS) per vehicle. In Q4, COGS per vehicle reached its lowest level ever.

gold.reference_context_note: COGS per vehicle record low (cost~COGS synonym).

corpus.evidence_text: A big part of the 4680 internal sales was a hedge against what would happen with our suppliers. For a while there, the price per kilowatt hour of lithium ion batteries went to crazy numbers, crazy levels.

gold.reference_context_note: SINGLETON: lithium battery-cost spike is real but rare. Defer OR admit battery_cost both score correct (K13).

corpus.evidence_text: The third quarter 2024 medical care ratio was 85.2% compared to 82.3% last year. Among factors contributing to the increase were the CMS Medicare funding reductions and member mix.

gold.reference_context_note: MCR rose to 85.2% on funding cuts + mix.

corpus.evidence_text: The first quarter medical care ratio was 84.8% compared to 84.3% in 2024, primarily due to ongoing Medicare funding reductions, member mix and higher senior care activity, partially offset by Medicare Part D program changes.

gold.reference_context_note: MCR rose again to 84.8%.

corpus.evidence_text: Our pricing assumptions were well short of actual medical costs. Physician and outpatient care together represent 70% of the pressure year to date. In short, most encounters are intensifying in services and costing more.

gold.reference_context_note: Cost trend ran ahead of pricing; 'encounters intensifying / costing more' is a LOW-OVERLAP phrasing of medical_cost_trend (K3, pairs with s29 medical_care_ratio cluster).

corpus.evidence_text: Approximately a million membership contractions for 2026 across MA, including both group and individual. We're exiting products, impacting about 600,000 members. Medical trend pressure is increasing the cost of healthcare, and the funding cuts to the program are degrading choice, access, and value to the consumer.

gold.reference_context_note: MA membership contracting ~1M from product exits; driver buried in a multi-cause hedge (trend + funding + choice).

corpus.evidence_text: UnitedHealthcare's CEO said pricing assumptions were well short of actual medical costs, expecting the full-year Medicare Advantage trend to be approximately 7.5%, well above the 5% trend it had assumed when setting 2025 bids.

gold.reference_context_note: Same cost-trend blowout via news — near-dup of s31 (distinct source, different wording).

corpus.evidence_text: Consolidated gross margin rate up 42bps led by Walmart U.S. Global eCommerce sales grew 21%, led by store-fulfilled pickup & delivery and marketplace. Walmart U.S. comp sales up 3.8%.

gold.reference_context_note: Comp +3.8%, e-comm +21%, GM +42bps.

corpus.evidence_text: Walmart U.S. comp sales up 5.3% with positive growth in general merchandise. eCommerce up 27% globally. Consolidated operating income up 8.2% due to higher gross margins and growth in membership income.

gold.reference_context_note: Comp +5.3%, e-comm +27%.

corpus.evidence_text: The improvement in gross margin, inventory is a big part of that. Inventory in the U.S. was down 4.5%. We saw markdowns in the quarter be notably less than they were the year before. And all those have an effect on gross margin.

gold.reference_context_note: Gross margin up on lower inventory/markdowns (the metric 'gross_margin' is named but the CAUSE words are inventory/markdowns -> don't coin those as drivers).

corpus.evidence_text: What was particularly helpful was the improvement in general merchandise as the quarter progressed, led by our e-commerce business and marketplace. Our e-commerce business has accelerated, the 26 percent growth rate, which compares to 20s in previous quarters.

gold.reference_context_note: E-comm accelerated to 26%.

corpus.evidence_text: Look, the consumer remains choiceful and value-focused, and there's a lot of uncertainty in the macro environment. We feel great about our strategy and our ability to navigate whatever comes. We're confident in the team.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED boilerplate: generic macro/confidence language — sounds important, no reusable driver (K7).

corpus.evidence_text: Adjusted total unit revenue (TRASM) was down 2.6 percent from the prior year. Adjusted fuel price of $2.64 per gallon increased 5 percent year-over-year.

gold.reference_context_note: TRASM (~unit_revenue synonym) -2.6%; fuel +5%.

corpus.evidence_text: December quarter revenue growth of 5.7 percent, ahead of guidance, on an acceleration in demand. Adjusted total unit revenue (TRASM) improved several points sequentially to up 0.4 percent year-over-year.

gold.reference_context_note: TRASM flipped positive (direction flip vs s39).

corpus.evidence_text: We are reducing planned capacity growth in the second half of the year to flat over last year while actively managing costs. The adjusted average fuel price was $2.47 per gallon, down from $2.79.

gold.reference_context_note: Capacity cut to flat amid stalled demand; fuel fell.

corpus.evidence_text: Third quarter was clearly disappointing, and I think it was a host of things. Some of it might have been our fault in terms of where we thought the booking curve would be and how we held out for higher fares. When the spring swoon was happening and everybody got nervous when tariffs were introduced, that was the booking window for the latter part of the summer.

gold.reference_context_note: Messy multi-cause hedge; the driver is weak unit_revenue from soft summer bookings.

corpus.evidence_text: Net revenues in FICC were $2.03 billion, 24% lower than the fourth quarter of 2022. Net revenues in Equities were $2.61 billion, 26% higher. Investment banking fees were $1.65 billion, 12% lower, due to a decline in industry-wide completed M&A volumes.

gold.reference_context_note: Mixed-direction same event: FICC/IB down, Equities up.

corpus.evidence_text: Investment banking fees increased 21% year-over-year to $1.73 billion, led by higher revenues in Debt and Equity underwriting, including leveraged finance and IPOs. Net revenues in FICC were $3.18 billion, up 17% Y/Y.

gold.reference_context_note: IB fees +21%, FICC +17%.

corpus.evidence_text: Net revenues in Equities were $4.30 billion, 36% higher. Net revenues in FICC were $3.47 billion, 9% higher. Investment banking fees were $2.19 billion, 26% higher, due to strength in Advisory.

gold.reference_context_note: Equities +36%, FICC +9%, IB +26%.

corpus.evidence_text: Asset and Wealth Management revenue declined 3% to $3.78 billion due to lower returns from equity and debt investments. Net Interest Income surged 56% to $3.10 billion, driven by lower funding costs.

gold.reference_context_note: AWM -3% while NII +56% (mixed direction, same event).

corpus.evidence_text: We've taken the traditional financing businesses and capital markets businesses, we've taken all the coverage of financial sponsors, and we're putting them together and bringing talent that understands both our own balance sheet and capital deployments. This is where we actually think there's going to be growth in the capital markets, and our ability to capture more of that share allows us to do better.

gold.reference_context_note: Forward strategic-narrative (a reorg they THINK will grow share) — no realized driver yet. Reject or defer (K7).

corpus.evidence_text: Management guided to third-quarter revenues with the mid-points below expectations. AWS is solidly in re-acceleration mode with growth at 19% and exceeding the Street outlook. Total retail growth decelerated, with core retail margins remaining below the pre-pandemic levels.

gold.reference_context_note: Mixed: AWS re-accelerating but retail decelerating + soft guidance (guidance itself = reject).

corpus.evidence_text: GM canceled their pedal-less, wheel-less vehicle. And according to the company this morning, their decision was driven by uncertainty about the regulatory environment.

gold.reference_context_note: A COMPETITOR's one-off product cancellation — not Tesla's own reusable driver (K6 + cross-entity reject).

corpus.evidence_text: Nvidia is set to beat Wall Street's expectations once again, driven by surging demand for AI and its new Blackwell architecture. The company's Data Center segment remains the primary growth engine.

gold.reference_context_note: Data Center the growth engine on AI/Blackwell demand.

corpus.evidence_text: A cyber incident at our technology unit disrupted claims processing for several weeks, which weighed on the quarter as we worked through the backlog and absorbed remediation costs.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED singleton: a real-but-rare operational shock (cyber-driven claims disruption). Defer OR admit both correct; low lexical overlap with any common driver (K13+K3).

corpus.evidence_text: We continued to see households making more deliberate choices, leaning into our opening price points and private brands, which carried our unit growth even as customers managed their budgets.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED paraphrase-evidence: 'private brands / opening price points / trading down' -> private_label_sales, where the canonical token is NOT literal (R11 stress, K15).

corpus.evidence_text: We have the best people in the industry, an incredible brand, and a durable competitive advantage. We're going to keep running the best operation and taking care of our customers, and the financial results will follow.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED boilerplate: generic strategy/brand confidence — no reusable driver (K7).

corpus.evidence_text: The Board of Directors authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $40 billion, with no fixed expiration date.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED one-off: a buyback authorization is a capital-return EVENT, not a causal operating driver (K6).

corpus.evidence_text: Colette Kress addressed concerns about gross margins, forecasting a start in the low 70% range during the first half of 2025, gradually increasing to mid-70% as Blackwell chip production stabilizes. Near-term hurdles, including delayed chip rollouts and margin pressures, are balanced by unprecedented demand in the AI and data center markets.

gold.reference_context_note: Near-term margin pressure (Blackwell ramp) offset by unprecedented DC demand — mixed direction + state-word-in-cause.

corpus.evidence_text: These are hyperscalers trying to deploy more and more capacity of AI data centers, and you start to hear them talk in terms of power. They don't even talk in terms of how many XPU or GPU classes, they talk in 500 megawatt, one gigawatt.

gold.reference_context_note: Demand framed in megawatts (uncommon); driver is AI accelerator demand. Paraphrase + novel framing + messy.

corpus.evidence_text: We're really pleased with the results this quarter, 20% year over year on an annualized run rate of $132 billion, and we see the growth in both inference and training, and in the use of our Trainium custom silicon.

gold.reference_context_note: AWS +20% on AI inference/training + Trainium (novel token); 'aws' paraphrased as 'the results this quarter'.

corpus.evidence_text: Quarter-to-date deliveries in the U.S. down mid-teens year-over-year. Registration data in Europe shows a 50% year-over-year decline for April. In China, demand is also declining based on key data points.

gold.reference_context_note: Deliveries declining across US/Europe/China (one driver, many regions).

corpus.evidence_text: A 17% annual decline in Apple's shipments in the country, its largest-ever sales drop in the market. Competition from Chinese manufacturers and a lack of AI integration in Apple's latest devices may have eroded its market position.

gold.reference_context_note: iPhone China -17% (largest-ever); the cause (AI gap + local competition) is NOT the driver name.

corpus.evidence_text: We expect strong demand for travel to continue, with consumers increasingly seeking premium products. Total passenger revenue for the quarter grew 5% year over year. Adjusted average fuel price was $2.34 per gallon, down from $3.00.

gold.reference_context_note: Strong premium demand; fuel fell.

corpus.evidence_text: A faulty CrowdStrike software update triggered a multi-day operational meltdown, forcing Delta to cancel thousands of flights, with management estimating roughly $500 million of direct costs and lost revenue in the quarter.

gold.reference_context_note: CONSTRUCTED from the real Jul-2024 CrowdStrike-Delta outage: a real-but-RARE operational shock. Defer OR admit operational_disruption both correct (K13); the vendor-named one-off should not be the canonical name.
